articles.title: Psychology textbooks still misrepresent famous experiments and controversial debates
articles.titleKo: 심리학 교과서가 여전히 유명한 실험과 논쟁적 논제를 잘못 표현하고 있다
articles.summary: College psychology textbooks still struggle to accurately present controversial topics and historical events, according to new research. While some improvements have occurred, textbooks frequently repeat scientific urban legends.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated how accurately college psychology textbooks present historical controversies and scientific events. The researchers examined multiple psychology textbooks and analyzed patterns of misinformation, finding that many textbooks misrepresent famous psychological experiments. Particularly notable is the finding that historically important experiments are being oversimplified, reinterpreted, and repeatedly perpetuated across textbooks. The study revealed that some textbooks present scientific "urban legends" as factual information. This is problematic because students learning psychology receive inaccurate information as established knowledge. The researchers emphasize that textbook editors and authors must prioritize historical accuracy in their work. However, the study has limitations as it focuses on a specific range of textbooks, making it difficult to generalize findings to all psychology textbooks.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대학 심리학 교과서가 역사적 논쟁과 과학적 사건들을 얼마나 정확하게 다루고 있는지 조사했습니다. 연구진은 여러 심리학 교과서를 검토하여 잘못된 정보의 패턴을 분석했으며, 많은 교과서가 유명한 실험들을 왜곡되게 표현하고 있음을 발견했습니다. 특히 역사적으로 중요한 심리학 실험들이 단순화되거나 잘못 해석되어 반복적으로 수록되고 있습니다. 연구 결과, 일부 교과서는 과학적 '도시전설'을 마치 사실인 양 전달하고 있었습니다. 이는 심리학을 배우는 학생들이 부정확한 정보를 학습하게 된다는 점에서 문제가 있습니다. 연구진은 교과서 편집자와 저자들이 역사적 정확성을 더욱 중시해야 한다고 강조합니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 교과서의 범위에 한정되어 있어 모든 교과서에 일반화하기는 어렵다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated how accurately college psychology textbooks present historical controversies and scientific events. The researchers examined multiple psychology textbooks and analyzed patterns of misinformation, finding that many textbooks misrepresent famous psychological experiments. Particularly notable is the finding that historically important experiments are being oversimplified, reinterpreted, and repeatedly perpetuated across textbooks. The study revealed that some textbooks present scientific "urban legends" as factual information. This is problematic because students learning psychology receive inaccurate information as established knowledge. The researchers emphasize that textbook editors and authors must prioritize historical accuracy in their work. However, the study has limitations as it focuses on a specific range of textbooks, making it difficult to generalize findings to all psychology textbooks.

##
keyPoints: **역사적 부정확성 / Historical Inaccuracy**: 심리학 교과서들이 유명한 실험과 과학적 논쟁을 왜곡되게 표현하고 있음
**과학적 도시전설 / Scientific Urban Legends**: 검증되지 않은 심리학 '통설'이 교과서에서 사실처럼 반복 수록되고 있음
**교육적 위험성 / Educational Risk**: 학생들이 부정확한 심리학 정보를 기초 지식으로 습득하게 되는 문제 발생
**개선의 필요성 / Need for Improvement**: 교과서 저자와 편집자의 역사적 정확성 강화 및 학술적 검증 체계 개선 필요

articles.title: Making podcasts instead of just listening to them might help medical students learn
articles.titleKo: 팟캐스트를 듣기만 하는 것이 아니라 직접 만들기가 의대생들의 학습을 도울 수 있다
articles.summary: Can recording a podcast help you ace an exam? Researchers tested if actively producing audio content improved medical students' knowledge of heart defects compared to passive listening, yielding promising but inconclusive results.
articles.aiSummary:
Researchers investigated whether actively producing podcast content could improve medical students' learning outcomes compared to passive listening, focusing on knowledge retention of heart defects. The study divided medical students into two groups: one that created podcasts on the subject and another that only listened to pre-recorded content, then measured their exam performance. Results showed that students who actively produced podcasts demonstrated better test scores than those who passively listened, but the difference was not statistically conclusive. This research suggests that active student engagement may enhance learning outcomes in medical education. However, the study has limitations including potentially small sample sizes and incomplete control of experimental variables. Further large-scale research is needed to definitively determine whether podcast production is an effective learning tool in medical education.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
의료 교육에서 학습 효과를 높이기 위한 새로운 방법을 찾기 위해 연구팀은 팟캐스트 제작 활동이 수동적 청취보다 효과적인지 검증했습니다. 의대 학생들을 대상으로 심장 결손증에 대해 팟캐스트를 직접 제작하는 그룹과 단순히 청취하는 그룹으로 나누어 실험을 진행했습니다. 연구 결과, 팟캐스트를 적극적으로 제작한 학생들이 시험 성적에서 더 나은 결과를 보였지만, 그 차이가 통계적으로 명확하지 않아 결론을 내리기는 아직 어렵다는 상황입니다. 이 연구는 학생들의 능동적 참여가 학습 성과를 향상시킬 수 있다는 가능성을 시사합니다. 다만 표본 크기가 제한적이거나 실험 설계의 변수 통제가 충분하지 않았을 수 있다는 한계가 있습니다. 추후 더 대규모의 연구를 통해 팟캐스트 제작이 의료 교육에서 효과적인 학습 도구인지 확실히 밝혀야 할 것으로 보입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Researchers investigated whether actively producing podcast content could improve medical students' learning outcomes compared to passive listening, focusing on knowledge retention of heart defects. The study divided medical students into two groups: one that created podcasts on the subject and another that only listened to pre-recorded content, then measured their exam performance. Results showed that students who actively produced podcasts demonstrated better test scores than those who passively listened, but the difference was not statistically conclusive. This research suggests that active student engagement may enhance learning outcomes in medical education. However, the study has limitations including potentially small sample sizes and incomplete control of experimental variables. Further large-scale research is needed to definitively determine whether podcast production is an effective learning tool in medical education.

##
keyPoints: 능동적 학습과 수동적 학습 비교: 팟캐스트 직접 제작 vs. 단순 청취의 학습 효과 차이 검증 / Active vs. passive learning: Testing the learning effectiveness difference between creating podcasts and simply listening
의료 교육의 새로운 교수법: 멀티미디어 콘텐츠 제작을 통한 의학 지식 습득 방식 제시 / New teaching method in medical education: Proposing knowledge acquisition through multimedia content creation
결과의 한계성: 통계적으로 유의미하지 않은 결과로 인한 결론의 불확실성 / Inconclusive results: Statistical insignificance preventing definitive conclusions
추가 연구의 필요성: 표본 확대 및 엄격한 실험 통제를 통한 후속 연구 필요 / Need for further research: Larger-scale studies with stricter experimental controls required

articles.title: New study suggests dreams function as a “multimotive simulation space”
articles.titleKo: 새로운 연구에 따르면 꿈은 "다목적 시뮬레이션 공간"으로 기능한다
articles.summary: New research indicates that dreams often act as a simulation for social hurdles. By analyzing hundreds of dream reports, scientists found that themes like status and self-protection frequently organize the content of our sleep narratives.
articles.aiSummary:
This study proposes that dreams function as a "multimotive simulation space" rather than meaningless brain activity—a space where we practice dealing with social challenges. Researchers conducted their investigation by collecting and systematically analyzing hundreds of dream reports. The analysis revealed that dream content is predominantly organized around two key themes: social status and self-protection mechanisms. These findings support the evolutionary psychology perspective that dreams allow humans to safely process threatening situations related to relationships and survival. The study's significance lies in providing scientific validation for the psychological functions of dreams, which can enhance our understanding of mental health. However, limitations include the need for further analysis of individual differences and cultural variations, as well as potential subjectivity in dream content interpretation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 꿈이 단순한 무의미한 뇌 활동이 아니라 우리가 직면한 사회적 문제들을 미리 연습하는 '다목적 시뮬레이션 공간'으로 기능한다는 가설을 검증했다. 연구진은 수백 건의 꿈 기록을 수집하여 체계적으로 분석하는 방식으로 실험을 진행했다. 분석 결과, 꿈의 내용은 사회적 지위(status)와 자기보호(self-protection)라는 두 가지 핵심 주제에 의해 조직되는 경향을 보였다. 이는 꿈이 인간관계와 생존에 관련된 위협 상황들을 안전한 환경에서 다룰 수 있도록 도와준다는 진화심리학적 관점을 지지한다. 본 연구의 의의는 꿈의 심리적 기능을 과학적으로 규명함으로써 정신건강 이해를 높였다는 점이다. 다만 개인차와 문화적 차이에 대한 추가 분석이 필요하며, 꿈의 내용 분석에 주관성이 개입될 수 있다는 한계가 있다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study proposes that dreams function as a "multimotive simulation space" rather than meaningless brain activity—a space where we practice dealing with social challenges. Researchers conducted their investigation by collecting and systematically analyzing hundreds of dream reports. The analysis revealed that dream content is predominantly organized around two key themes: social status and self-protection mechanisms. These findings support the evolutionary psychology perspective that dreams allow humans to safely process threatening situations related to relationships and survival. The study's significance lies in providing scientific validation for the psychological functions of dreams, which can enhance our understanding of mental health. However, limitations include the need for further analysis of individual differences and cultural variations, as well as potential subjectivity in dream content interpretation.

##
keyPoints: **꿈의 기능성** / Dreams serve functional purposes: 꿈은 단순 뇌 활동이 아닌 사회적 도전 상황을 시뮬레이션하는 공간으로 기능한다. Dreams are not random but function as simulations for navigating social challenges.
**조직화된 주제** / Organized thematic content: 꿈의 내용은 사회적 지위와 자기보호라는 명확한 주제 중심으로 조직되어 있다. Dream narratives are systematically organized around themes of status and self-protection.

**진화심리학적 의미** / Evolutionary significance: 이러한 꿈의 메커니즘은 인간의 생존과 사회적 적응에 필수적인 위협 대처 능력을 발달시켰을 가능성이 있다. Dreams may have evolved as a mechanism to develop threat-coping abilities essential for survival and social adaptation.
**추가 연구 필요** / Need for further research: 개인차, 문화적 차이, 그리고 해석의 객관성 문제 등 보완이 필요하다. Individual differences, cultural variations, and interpretative objectivity require further investigation.

articles.title: These 80-year-olds have the memory of 50-year-olds. Scientists now know why
articles.titleKo: 80세인데 50대의 기억력을 가진 사람들, 과학자들이 그 이유를 밝혀내다
articles.summary: A rare group of adults over 80, known as SuperAgers, are rewriting what we thought was possible for the aging brain. With memory abilities comparable to people decades younger, their brains either resist or withstand the damage typically linked to Alzheimer’s disease. Decades of research reveal that their social lifestyles and unique brain biology may hold the key to preserving cognitive function. Scientists believe these insights could pave the way for new strategies to delay or even prevent dementia.
articles.aiSummary:
This research focuses on "SuperAgers," a rare group of adults over 80 who maintain memory abilities comparable to people in their 50s, challenging conventional understanding of cognitive aging. These individuals possess unique brain biology that allows them to resist or withstand brain damage typically associated with Alzheimer's disease. Through decades of research, scientists have identified that their active social lifestyles and distinctive brain characteristics may be key factors in preserving cognitive function. The findings suggest that these insights could lead to the development of new strategies to delay or prevent dementia in the general population. However, as this is an observational study of a specific population group, additional validation is needed before these findings can be directly applied to the broader aging population.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 80세 이상의 나이에도 50대 수준의 기억력을 유지하는 '슈퍼에이저(SuperAgers)'라 불리는 특별한 집단에 관한 것이다. 이들은 일반적으로 노화에 따른 알츠하이머병 관련 뇌 손상에 저항하거나 그 영향을 견디어낼 수 있는 독특한 뇌 생물학적 특성을 가지고 있다. 수십 년의 연구를 통해 과학자들은 이들의 활발한 사회생활과 뇌의 구조적 특징이 인지 기능 보존의 핵심일 수 있음을 발견했다. 이러한 연구 결과는 치매를 지연시키거나 예방하기 위한 새로운 전략 개발에 기여할 것으로 기대된다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 집단을 대상으로 한 관찰 연구이므로, 일반 인구에 대한 직접적인 적용에는 추가 검증이 필요하다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research focuses on "SuperAgers," a rare group of adults over 80 who maintain memory abilities comparable to people in their 50s, challenging conventional understanding of cognitive aging. These individuals possess unique brain biology that allows them to resist or withstand brain damage typically associated with Alzheimer's disease. Through decades of research, scientists have identified that their active social lifestyles and distinctive brain characteristics may be key factors in preserving cognitive function. The findings suggest that these insights could lead to the development of new strategies to delay or prevent dementia in the general population. However, as this is an observational study of a specific population group, additional validation is needed before these findings can be directly applied to the broader aging population.

##
keyPoints: **슈퍼에이저의 발견** / Discovery of SuperAgers: 80세 이상 중 50대 수준의 기억력을 유지하는 특별한 집단이 존재하며, 이는 뇌의 특이한 생물학적 구조로 인한 현상이다.
**뇌 손상 저항성** / Brain Damage Resistance: 슈퍼에이저들의 뇌는 알츠하이머병과 관련된 일반적인 노화성 손상에 효과적으로 저항하거나 견딜 수 있다.
**사회생활의 역할** / Role of Social Engagement: 활발한 사회적 상호작용과 생활양식이 인지 기능 보존의 중요한 요소로 작용한다.
**치매 예방의 실마리** / Clue to Dementia Prevention: 슈퍼에이저 연구를 통해 얻은 통찰력이 향후 치매 지연 및 예방 전략 개발의 기초가 될 수 있다.

articles.title: Scientists say this type of olive oil could boost brain power
articles.titleKo: 과학자들, 이 종류의 올리브유가 뇌 기능을 향상시킬 수 있다고 밝혀
articles.summary: Extra virgin olive oil might help protect your brain by working through your gut. A two-year study found that people who consumed it had better cognitive performance and more diverse gut bacteria than those using refined olive oil. Researchers even identified specific microbes linked to these benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality olive oil could be a simple way to support brain health as you age.
articles.aiSummary:
This research investigated whether extra virgin olive oil could protect brain health through gut microbiota. Researchers conducted a two-year study comparing cognitive performance and gut bacterial diversity between people consuming extra virgin olive oil and those using refined olive oil. Results showed that the extra virgin olive oil group demonstrated better cognitive performance and significantly more diverse gut bacteria. Researchers successfully identified specific microbes associated with these cognitive benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality extra virgin olive oil could be a simple dietary strategy to support brain health during aging. However, further research is needed to establish the causal relationship between specific gut microbes and cognitive improvement.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 엑스트라 버진 올리브유가 장(gut)을 통해 뇌 건강을 보호할 수 있다는 가설에서 출발했습니다. 연구진은 2년에 걸쳐 엑스트라 버진 올리브유를 섭취한 사람들과 정제 올리브유를 사용한 사람들을 비교하는 실험을 진행했습니다. 결과적으로 엑스트라 버진 올리브유 섭취 그룹이 더 나은 인지 능력과 더 다양한 장내 박테리아를 보유한 것으로 나타났습니다. 연구팀은 이러한 뇌 건강 개선 효과와 연관된 특정 미생물들을 특정할 수 있었습니다. 이 발견은 고품질 올리브유 선택이 노화에 따른 뇌 건강 유지의 간단한 방법이 될 수 있음을 시사합니다. 다만 이 연구는 장내 미생물과 뇌 건강의 인과관계를 명확히 하기 위한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research investigated whether extra virgin olive oil could protect brain health through gut microbiota. Researchers conducted a two-year study comparing cognitive performance and gut bacterial diversity between people consuming extra virgin olive oil and those using refined olive oil. Results showed that the extra virgin olive oil group demonstrated better cognitive performance and significantly more diverse gut bacteria. Researchers successfully identified specific microbes associated with these cognitive benefits. The findings suggest that choosing high-quality extra virgin olive oil could be a simple dietary strategy to support brain health during aging. However, further research is needed to establish the causal relationship between specific gut microbes and cognitive improvement.

##
keyPoints: **장-뇌 축의 중요성** / Gut-brain axis importance: 엑스트라 버진 올리브유가 장내 미생물 다양성 증가를 통해 인지 기능 개선에 영향을 미침
**인지 성능 향상** / Cognitive performance improvement: 엑스트라 버진 올리브유 섭취자들이 정제 올리브유 사용자보다 더 나은 인지 능력 수행 결과를 보임
**특정 미생물 규명** / Specific microbe identification: 뇌 건강 개선과 직접 연관된 특정 장내 미생물을 과학적으로 특정할 수 있었음
**예방적 식이 전략** / Preventive dietary strategy: 고품질 올리브유 선택이 노화 과정에서 뇌 건강을 유지하는 간단하고 실질적인 방법으로 제시됨

articles.title: The Tool-Overuse Illusion: Why Does LLM Prefer External Tools over Internal Knowledge?
articles.titleKo: LLM의 도구 과다사용 착각: 왜 대규모 언어모델은 내부 지식보다 외부 도구를 선호할까?
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.19749v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Equipping LLMs with external tools effectively addresses internal reasoning limitations. However, it introduces a critical yet under-explored phenomenon: tool overuse, the unnecessary tool-use during reasoning. In this paper, we first reveal this phenomenon is pervasive across diverse LLMs. We then experimentally elucidate its underlying mechanisms through two key lenses: (1) First, by analyzing tool-use behavior across different internal knowledge availability regions, we identify a \textit{knowledge epistemic illusion}: models misjudge internal
articles.aiSummary:
Recent advances have shown that equipping large language models (LLMs) with external tools like search engines and calculators improves performance. However, researchers discovered a paradoxical phenomenon: models unnecessarily use external tools even when they already possess the required knowledge internally. This study revealed that tool overuse is prevalent across diverse LLMs including GPT and Claude. Through experiments manipulating internal knowledge availability, the research team identified the root cause as "knowledge epistemic illusion"—models incorrectly misjudge their own knowledge capabilities. This finding has significant practical implications, as unnecessary tool calls increase computational costs and response latency in real-world deployment. However, the study has limitations in analyzing how this phenomenon varies across different task types and tool categories.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
최근 대규모 언어모델(LLM)에 검색 엔진이나 계산기 같은 외부 도구를 연결하면 모델의 성능이 향상된다고 알려져 있습니다. 그러나 연구팀은 흥미로운 역설을 발견했습니다: 모델들이 자신이 이미 알고 있는 내용까지도 외부 도구를 사용해서 해결하려는 '도구 과다 사용' 현상이 나타난다는 것입니다. 이 연구는 GPT, Claude 등 다양한 LLM에서 이 현상이 광범위하게 발생함을 확인했습니다. 연구팀은 모델의 내부 지식 접근성 수준을 달리하는 실험을 통해 근본 원인을 분석했으며, 모델들이 자신의 지식에 대해 잘못된 판단을 하는 '지식 인식 착각(epistemic illusion)'이 주요 원인임을 밝혔습니다. 이 발견은 LLM을 실제로 배포할 때 불필요한 도구 호출로 인한 비용 증가와 응답 지연을 초래할 수 있다는 점에서 중요합니다. 다만 이 연구는 특정 작업 유형이나 도구 종류에 따른 차이를 충분히 분석하지 못한 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Recent advances have shown that equipping large language models (LLMs) with external tools like search engines and calculators improves performance. However, researchers discovered a paradoxical phenomenon: models unnecessarily use external tools even when they already possess the required knowledge internally. This study revealed that tool overuse is prevalent across diverse LLMs including GPT and Claude. Through experiments manipulating internal knowledge availability, the research team identified the root cause as "knowledge epistemic illusion"—models incorrectly misjudge their own knowledge capabilities. This finding has significant practical implications, as unnecessary tool calls increase computational costs and response latency in real-world deployment. However, the study has limitations in analyzing how this phenomenon varies across different task types and tool categories.

##
keyPoints: **도구 과다 사용 현상**: LLM이 자신이 알고 있는 정보까지도 외부 도구를 통해 검색하려는 불필요한 행동이 광범위하게 발생 / Tool overuse phenomenon: LLMs unnecessarily seek external tools for information they already possess, occurring across diverse models

**근본 원인은 자기 지식 오판**: 모델들이 자신의 내부 지식 범위와 신뢰도를 정확히 인식하지 못하는 '지식 인식 착각'이 주요 메커니즘 / Root cause of knowledge misjudgment: Models suffer from epistemic illusion, failing to accurately assess their own knowledge scope and confidence levels
**실무적 비용 증가**: 불필요한 도구 호출은 API 호출 비용 증가, 응답 지연, 그리고 외부 시스템 부하 증가를 야기 / Practical cost implications: Unnecessary tool calls increase API costs, response latency, and strain on external systems

**다양한 LLM에서 일관된 패턴**: GPT, Claude 등 여러 주요 모델에서 동일한 도구 과다 사용 현상이 관찰되어 이것이 구조적 문제임을 시사 / Consistent pattern across models: The phenomenon observed in major LLMs like GPT and Claude suggests this is a structural issue rather than isolated problem

articles.title: AI to Learn 2.0: A Deliverable-Oriented Governance Framework and Maturity Rubric for Opaque AI in Learning-Intensive Domains
articles.titleKo: AI to Learn 2.0: 학습집약적 영역의 불투명한 AI를 위한 산출물 중심의 거버넌스 프레임워크 및 성숙도 척도
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.19751v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative AI is entering research, education, and professional work faster than current governance frameworks can specify how AI-assisted outputs should be judged in learning-intensive settings. The central problem is proxy failure: a polished artifact can be useful while no longer serving as credible evidence of the human understanding, judgment, or transfer ability that the work is supposed to cultivate or certify. This paper proposes AI to Learn 2.0, a deliverable-oriented governance framework for AI-assisted work. Rather than claiming elemen
articles.aiSummary: Generative AI is entering research, education, and professional work faster than current governance frameworks can guide how AI-assisted outputs should be evaluated in learning-intensive settings. The central problem is "proxy failure": while polished AI-generated artifacts can be useful, they no longer reliably demonstrate the human understanding, judgment, or learning ability that educational and professional work is meant to cultivate or certify. This paper proposes "AI to Learn 2.0," a deliverable-oriented governance framework for AI-assisted work that goes beyond simply regulating AI use. Instead, it provides evaluation criteria and a maturity rubric centered on whether actual learning objectives are achieved in these domains. By offering consistent assessment standards and developmental stages for managing AI use in education and professional contexts, the framework helps institutions implement coherent governance strategies. However, as an early-stage proposal, the research requires additional validation through real-world implementation across diverse learning domains and fields.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 생성형 AI가 연구, 교육, 전문 업무 영역에 빠르게 도입되고 있지만, 학습 중심 분야에서 AI 보조 결과물을 어떻게 평가할지에 대한 명확한 거버넌스 프레임워크가 부족한 상황이다. 이 연구의 핵심 문제는 '대리 실패(proxy failure)'인데, AI가 만든 고급스러운 결과물이 유용할 수 있지만 더 이상 학생이나 전문가의 실제 이해도, 판단력, 학습 능력을 보여주는 신뢰할 수 있는 증거가 되지 못한다는 의미이다. 논문은 AI 보조 작업을 위한 '결과물 중심 거버넌스 프레임워크(AI to Learn 2.0)'를 제안한다. 이 프레임워크는 단순히 AI 사용 여부를 판단하는 것이 아니라, 실제 학습 목표 달성 여부를 중심으로 평가 기준을 제시한다. 특히 교육과 전문 영역에서 AI 시대에 적절한 평가 기준과 성숙도 척도(maturity rubric)를 제공함으로써, 기관들이 일관된 방식으로 AI 활용을 관리할 수 있도록 돕는다. 다만 연구는 아직 초기 단계이며, 다양한 학습 영역에서의 실제 적용 사례와 검증이 추가로 필요하다는 한계가 있다.
articles.aiSummaryEn: Generative AI is entering research, education, and professional work faster than current governance frameworks can guide how AI-assisted outputs should be evaluated in learning-intensive settings. The central problem is "proxy failure": while polished AI-generated artifacts can be useful, they no longer reliably demonstrate the human understanding, judgment, or learning ability that educational and professional work is meant to cultivate or certify. This paper proposes "AI to Learn 2.0," a deliverable-oriented governance framework for AI-assisted work that goes beyond simply regulating AI use. Instead, it provides evaluation criteria and a maturity rubric centered on whether actual learning objectives are achieved in these domains. By offering consistent assessment standards and developmental stages for managing AI use in education and professional contexts, the framework helps institutions implement coherent governance strategies. However, as an early-stage proposal, the research requires additional validation through real-world implementation across diverse learning domains and fields.
keyPoints: **대리 실패 문제 / Proxy Failure Problem**
**결과물 중심 평가 / Deliverable-Oriented Assessment**
**성숙도 척도 제시 / Maturity Rubric Framework**
**학습 집약 분야 적용 / Application in Learning-Intensive Domains**

articles.title: Algorithm Selection with Zero Domain Knowledge via Text Embeddings
articles.titleKo: 텍스트 임베딩을 통한 영역 지식 없는 알고리즘 선택
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.19753v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a feature-free approach to algorithm selection that replaces hand-crafted instance features with pretrained text embeddings. Our method, ZeroFolio, proceeds in three steps: it reads the raw instance file as plain text, embeds it with a pretrained embedding model, and selects an algorithm via weighted k-nearest neighbors. The key to our approach is the observation that pretrained embeddings produce representations that distinguish problem instances without any domain knowledge or task-specific training. This allows us to apply the same
articles.aiSummary:
Algorithm selection—the task of automatically identifying the best algorithm for a given dataset—is an important challenge in machine learning. Traditional methods require complex manual feature extraction from data, but this research proposes ZeroFolio, a novel approach using pretrained text embeddings. ZeroFolio operates in three steps: reading raw data files as plain text, converting them with a pretrained embedding model, and selecting the optimal algorithm using weighted k-nearest neighbors. The key insight is that pretrained embeddings can distinguish problem instances without domain-specific knowledge or task-specific training. This offers versatility by enabling the same model to be applied across diverse domains. The research's significance lies in automating algorithm selection without complicated feature engineering. However, further validation across diverse data formats and domains is needed to establish the method's broader applicability.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
알고리즘 선택 문제는 주어진 데이터에 가장 적합한 알고리즘을 자동으로 찾는 머신러닝 분야의 중요한 과제입니다. 기존 방식은 데이터의 특성을 수동으로 추출하는 복잡한 과정이 필요했으나, 본 연구는 사전학습된 텍스트 임베딩을 활용하는 'ZeroFolio'라는 새로운 방법을 제안합니다. ZeroFolio는 세 단계로 작동합니다: 원본 데이터 파일을 일반 텍스트로 읽고, 사전학습된 임베딩 모델로 변환한 후, 가중치가 적용된 k-최근접 이웃 알고리즘으로 최적의 알고리즘을 선택합니다. 이 접근 방식의 핵심은 사전학습된 임베딩이 특별한 도메인 지식이나 작업별 학습 없이도 데이터의 특성을 구별할 수 있다는 발견입니다. 이는 다양한 분야에 동일한 모델을 적용할 수 있는 범용성을 제공합니다. 연구의 의의는 복잡한 특성 엔지니어링 없이 자동으로 알고리즘을 선택할 수 있다는 점이며, 향후 더 다양한 데이터 형식과 도메인에 대한 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Algorithm selection—the task of automatically identifying the best algorithm for a given dataset—is an important challenge in machine learning. Traditional methods require complex manual feature extraction from data, but this research proposes ZeroFolio, a novel approach using pretrained text embeddings. ZeroFolio operates in three steps: reading raw data files as plain text, converting them with a pretrained embedding model, and selecting the optimal algorithm using weighted k-nearest neighbors. The key insight is that pretrained embeddings can distinguish problem instances without domain-specific knowledge or task-specific training. This offers versatility by enabling the same model to be applied across diverse domains. The research's significance lies in automating algorithm selection without complicated feature engineering. However, further validation across diverse data formats and domains is needed to establish the method's broader applicability.

##
keyPoints: **도메인 지식 불필요의 혁신** / Revolutionary Elimination of Domain Knowledge Requirements
**사전학습된 임베딩의 활용** / Leverage of Pretrained Embeddings
**범용적 적용 가능성** / Cross-Domain Applicability
**계산 효율성** / Computational Efficiency

articles.title: Exploring Data Augmentation and Resampling Strategies for Transformer-Based Models to Address Class Imbalance in AI Scoring of Scientific Explanations in NGSS Classroom
articles.titleKo: NGSS 교실에서 과학 설명의 AI 채점 시 클래스 불균형 해결을 위한 트랜스포머 기반 모델의 데이터 증강 및 리샘플링 전략 탐색
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.19754v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Automated scoring of students' scientific explanations offers the potential for immediate, accurate feedback, yet class imbalance in rubric categories particularly those capturing advanced reasoning remains a challenge. This study investigates augmentation strategies to improve transformer-based text classification of student responses to a physical science assessment based on an NGSS-aligned learning progression. The dataset consists of 1,466 high school responses scored on 11 binary-coded analytic categories. This rubric identifies six importan
articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the challenge of automated scoring of students' scientific explanations, particularly the class imbalance problem affecting the assessment of advanced reasoning skills. The research investigates data augmentation and resampling strategies for transformer-based text classification models using a dataset of 1,466 high school student responses scored across 11 binary-coded analytic rubric categories. The evaluation is based on an NGSS-aligned (Next Generation Science Standards) learning progression for physical science assessment. Researchers implemented various augmentation techniques to improve model performance, especially for underrepresented categories capturing higher-order thinking. Key findings demonstrate that specific augmentation strategies significantly improved the identification rate of minority classes in the rubric categories. The research has practical significance for enhancing fairness and accuracy in automated scoring systems that provide immediate feedback to students. However, limitations include the single assessment context and relatively constrained dataset that may limit generalizability across different subjects or educational settings.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 고등학교 학생들의 과학 설명에 대한 자동 채점 시스템 개발의 문제점을 해결하기 위해 진행되었습니다. 특히 고급 추론 능력을 평가하는 항목들이 데이터 불균형 문제를 야기하여, 인공지능 모델의 정확도가 떨어지는 것이 주요 배경입니다. 연구팀은 고등학생 1,466명의 응답을 11개의 이진 분류 항목으로 채점한 데이터를 수집하고, 트랜스포머 기반의 텍스트 분류 모델에 다양한 데이터 증강 및 리샘플링 전략을 적용했습니다. 이 연구는 NGSS(미국 과학 교육 표준) 기반의 학습 진행 평가에 중점을 두고 있습니다. 주요 결과로는 특정 증강 전략들이 소수 클래스 데이터의 인식률을 유의미하게 향상시켰음을 보여줍니다. 이 연구는 자동 채점 시스템의 공정성과 정확성을 개선할 수 있는 실무적 방안을 제시한다는 점에서 의의가 있습니다. 다만 단일 평가 맥락과 제한된 데이터셋이라는 한계를 가지고 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study addresses the challenge of automated scoring of students' scientific explanations, particularly the class imbalance problem affecting the assessment of advanced reasoning skills. The research investigates data augmentation and resampling strategies for transformer-based text classification models using a dataset of 1,466 high school student responses scored across 11 binary-coded analytic rubric categories. The evaluation is based on an NGSS-aligned (Next Generation Science Standards) learning progression for physical science assessment. Researchers implemented various augmentation techniques to improve model performance, especially for underrepresented categories capturing higher-order thinking. Key findings demonstrate that specific augmentation strategies significantly improved the identification rate of minority classes in the rubric categories. The research has practical significance for enhancing fairness and accuracy in automated scoring systems that provide immediate feedback to students. However, limitations include the single assessment context and relatively constrained dataset that may limit generalizability across different subjects or educational settings.

##
keyPoints: **클래스 불균형 문제** / Class Imbalance Challenge: 고등 추론 능력을 평가하는 항목들의 데이터 부족으로 인한 자동 채점 모델의 성능 저하
The underrepresentation of samples in advanced reasoning categories causes automated scoring models to perform poorly on these important assessment dimensions
**트랜스포머 기반 모델 활용** / Transformer-Based Approach: 최신 AI 기술(트랜스포머)을 적용하여 학생들의 텍스트 기반 과학 설명을 자동으로 분류 및 평가
Leveraging state-of-the-art transformer architecture for automated text classification of student scientific explanations

articles.title: Frequency-Forcing: From Scaling-as-Time to Soft Frequency Guidance
articles.titleKo: 주파수 강제화: 시간-스케일링에서 소프트 주파수 유도까지
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.20902v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While standard flow-matching models transport noise to data uniformly, incorporating an explicit generation order - specifically, establishing coarse, low-frequency structure before fine detail - has proven highly effective for synthesizing natural images. Two recent works offer distinct paradigms for this. K-Flow imposes a hard frequency constraint by reinterpreting a frequency scaling variable as flow time, running the trajectory inside a transformed amplitude space. Latent Forcing provides a soft ordering mechanism by coupling the pixel flow w
articles.aiSummary:
This research presents a novel methodology to enhance the quality of AI-generated images by controlling the generation order of visual features. While standard flow-matching models uniformly transform noise into data, the researchers found that explicitly establishing coarse, low-frequency structures before fine details significantly improves natural image synthesis. The study proposes "Frequency-Forcing," a new framework that integrates two recent approaches: K-Flow, which imposes hard frequency constraints by reinterpreting a frequency scaling variable as flow time, and Latent Forcing, which provides soft ordering mechanisms by coupling pixel flows. This unified approach demonstrates superior effectiveness in synthesizing natural images compared to conventional methods. However, as an early-stage preprint, the research requires further validation across diverse conditions and investigation into practical applications before broader implementation.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 이미지 생성 AI의 품질을 향상시키기 위한 새로운 방법론을 제시합니다. 기존의 표준 flow-matching 모델은 노이즈를 데이터로 균일하게 변환하는 방식을 사용했으나, 연구팀은 먼저 큰 틀(저주파)부터 생성한 후 세부 사항(고주파)을 추가하는 순서를 명시적으로 도입할 필요성을 발견했습니다. 이번 연구는 K-Flow와 Latent Forcing이라는 두 가지 최근 접근방식을 통합하여 '빈도 강제(Frequency-Forcing)'라는 새로운 프레임워크를 제안합니다. K-Flow는 주파수 변수를 흐름 시간으로 재해석하여 엄격한 제약을 가하는 방식이고, Latent Forcing은 픽셀 흐름을 결합하여 부드러운 순서 메커니즘을 제공합니다. 이 통합 방식은 자연 이미지 합성에서 뛰어난 효과를 보여주었습니다. 다만 이 연구는 아직 초기 단계의 논문으로, 다양한 조건에서의 성능 검증과 실제 응용 가능성에 대한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research presents a novel methodology to enhance the quality of AI-generated images by controlling the generation order of visual features. While standard flow-matching models uniformly transform noise into data, the researchers found that explicitly establishing coarse, low-frequency structures before fine details significantly improves natural image synthesis. The study proposes "Frequency-Forcing," a new framework that integrates two recent approaches: K-Flow, which imposes hard frequency constraints by reinterpreting a frequency scaling variable as flow time, and Latent Forcing, which provides soft ordering mechanisms by coupling pixel flows. This unified approach demonstrates superior effectiveness in synthesizing natural images compared to conventional methods. However, as an early-stage preprint, the research requires further validation across diverse conditions and investigation into practical applications before broader implementation.

##
keyPoints: **생성 순서의 중요성** / **Importance of Generation Order**: 이미지 생성 시 저주파(큰 틀)부터 고주파(세부)로 순차적으로 생성하는 것이 품질 향상에 효과적임 / Synthesizing images by sequentially generating low-frequency (coarse structure) before high-frequency (fine details) significantly improves generation quality.

**두 가지 접근방식의 통합** / **Integration of Two Paradigms**: K-Flow의 엄격한 제약과 Latent Forcing의 유연한 메커니즘을 결합하여 최적의 성능 달성 / Combining K-Flow's hard constraints with Latent Forcing's flexible mechanisms achieves optimal performance balance.

**자연 이미지 합성 개선** / **Natural Image Synthesis Enhancement**: Frequency-Forcing 프레임워크가 기존 flow-matching 모델보다 우수한 이미지 생성 결과 제공 / The proposed framework delivers superior image generation results compared to standard flow-matching approaches.

**추가 검증 필요** / **Need for Further Validation**: 다양한 데이터셋과 실제 응용 환경에서의 성능 검증 및 계산 효율성 평가가 요구됨 / Validation across diverse datasets, real-world applications, and computational efficiency assessment are necessary before practical deployment.

articles.title: Reinforcing privacy reasoning in LLMs via normative simulacra from fiction
articles.titleKo: 픽션의 규범적 시뮬라크라를 통한 대규모 언어모델의 개인정보 보호 추론 강화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.20904v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Information handling practices of LLM agents are broadly misaligned with the contextual privacy expectations of their users. Contextual Integrity (CI) provides a principled framework, defining privacy as the appropriate flow of information within context-relative norms. However, existing approaches either double inference cost via supervisor-assistant architectures, or fine-tune on narrow task-specific data. We propose extracting normative simulacra (structured representations of norms and information flows) from fiction novels and using them to
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the misalignment between large language models' (LLM) information handling practices and users' contextual privacy expectations. Utilizing Contextual Integrity theory, researchers proposed extracting normative simulacra—structured representations of norms and information flows—from fiction novels to train LLMs for better privacy reasoning. Existing approaches have limitations: supervisor-assistant architectures double inference costs, while task-specific fine-tuning lacks generalizability. By leveraging rich fictional narratives as training sources, this method enables more efficient and generalizable privacy-aware reasoning without excessive computational overhead. The research is significant for improving LLM compliance with privacy policies and context-appropriate information handling. However, limitations include questions about the realism of fictional data and whether it adequately represents diverse cultural privacy norms across different contexts.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 대규모언어모델(LLM)이 사용자의 맥락적 개인정보보호 기대치와 맞지 않는 정보 처리 방식을 개선하기 위해 진행되었습니다. 연구진은 맥락적 무결성(Contextual Integrity) 이론을 적용하여, 소설에서 추출한 규범적 시뮬라크라(규범과 정보 흐름의 구조화된 표현)를 LLM에 학습시키는 방법을 제안했습니다. 기존 감독자-조수 구조는 추론 비용을 두 배로 증가시키거나 좁은 작업 데이터로만 미세조정되는 한계가 있었습니다. 본 연구는 픽션 소설이라는 풍부한 자료에서 추출한 규범적 시뮬라크라를 활용함으로써 더 효율적이고 일반화된 개인정보보호 추론을 가능하게 합니다. 이는 LLM의 개인정보보호 정책 준수를 개선하고 맥락에 맞는 정보 처리를 강화하는 데 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 소설 데이터의 현실성과 다양한 문화적 맥락의 포괄성에 대한 검증이 필요합니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This research addresses the misalignment between large language models' (LLM) information handling practices and users' contextual privacy expectations. Utilizing Contextual Integrity theory, researchers proposed extracting normative simulacra—structured representations of norms and information flows—from fiction novels to train LLMs for better privacy reasoning. Existing approaches have limitations: supervisor-assistant architectures double inference costs, while task-specific fine-tuning lacks generalizability. By leveraging rich fictional narratives as training sources, this method enables more efficient and generalizable privacy-aware reasoning without excessive computational overhead. The research is significant for improving LLM compliance with privacy policies and context-appropriate information handling. However, limitations include questions about the realism of fictional data and whether it adequately represents diverse cultural privacy norms across different contexts.

##
keyPoints: **개인정보보호 문제**: LLM의 정보 처리 방식이 사용자의 맥락적 개인정보보호 기대치와 불일치하는 문제 해결 필요 / LLMs' information handling practices are misaligned with contextual privacy expectations
**혁신적 학습 방법**: 소설 텍스트에서 추출한 규범적 시뮬라크라를 활용하여 효율적인 개인정보보호 추론 구현 / Using normative simulacra from fiction novels enables efficient privacy reasoning without doubling inference costs
**이론적 기초**: 맥락적 무결성(Contextual Integrity) 프레임워크로 개인정보보호를 맥락 내 정보 흐름의 적절성으로 정의 / Contextual Integrity framework defines privacy as appropriate information flow within context-specific norms
**연구의 한계**: 소설 데이터의 현실성 및 다양한 문화적·사회적 개인정보보호 규범 포괄 가능성 검증 필요 / Validation needed regarding realism of fictional data and representation of diverse cultural privacy norms

articles.title: Do Masked Autoencoders Improve Downhole Prediction? An Empirical Study on Real Well Drilling Data
articles.titleKo: 마스크된 오토인코더가 시추공 예측을 향상시키는가? 실제 유정 시추 데이터에 대한 실증 연구
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.20909v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Downhole drilling telemetry presents a fundamental labeling asymmetry: surface sensor data are generated continuously at 1~Hz, while labeled downhole measurements are costly, intermittent, and scarce. Current machine learning approaches for downhole metric prediction universally adopt fully supervised training from scratch, which is poorly suited to this data regime. We present the first empirical evaluation of masked autoencoder (MAE) pretraining for downhole drilling metric prediction. Using two publicly available Utah FORGE geothermal wells co
articles.aiSummary:
This study presents the first empirical evaluation of masked autoencoder (MAE) pretraining for downhole drilling metric prediction, addressing a critical data asymmetry problem in the drilling industry. Downhole drilling telemetry exhibits a fundamental labeling asymmetry where surface sensors generate continuous data at 1 Hz frequency, while labeled downhole measurements are costly, intermittent, and extremely scarce. Using publicly available data from two Utah FORGE geothermal wells, researchers evaluated whether MAE pretraining outperforms conventional fully supervised learning approaches. Masked autoencoders use self-supervised learning by intentionally masking portions of input data and learning to reconstruct complete patterns from remaining information, making them well-suited for data-scarce scenarios. The significance of this work lies in improving machine learning approaches for the oil, gas, and geothermal industries to optimize drilling performance and cost-efficiency. However, limitations include the restricted dataset from only two wells and potential variations in geological characteristics and drilling conditions across different regions that could affect generalizability.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
본 연구는 시추공 내부(downhole) 데이터 예측에 마스크된 오토인코더(MAE) 사전학습 기법이 효과적인지를 평가한 첫 번째 경험적 연구입니다. 시추 작업에서 지표면 센서는 초당 1회 빈도로 지속적으로 데이터를 생성하지만, 시추공 내부의 측정값은 비용이 많이 들고 드물게 수집되어 레이블된 데이터가 심각하게 부족한 상황입니다. 연구팀은 미국 유타 주의 FORGE 지열정 2개 우물의 공개 데이터를 활용하여 MAE 사전학습이 완전 지도학습 대비 얼마나 성능을 개선하는지 검증했습니다. 마스크된 오토인코더는 입력 데이터의 일부를 의도적으로 숨긴 후 나머지 데이터만으로 전체 패턴을 학습하는 자기지도학습 기법으로, 레이블 부족 상황에서 효과적입니다. 이 연구는 석유·가스·지열 산업에서 시추 성능 최적화에 필요한 머신러닝 접근법의 한계를 개선하는 데 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 2개 우물의 제한된 데이터셋만 사용했고 다른 지역의 지질학적 특성이나 시추 조건이 다를 수 있다는 점이 한계입니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study presents the first empirical evaluation of masked autoencoder (MAE) pretraining for downhole drilling metric prediction, addressing a critical data asymmetry problem in the drilling industry. Downhole drilling telemetry exhibits a fundamental labeling asymmetry where surface sensors generate continuous data at 1 Hz frequency, while labeled downhole measurements are costly, intermittent, and extremely scarce. Using publicly available data from two Utah FORGE geothermal wells, researchers evaluated whether MAE pretraining outperforms conventional fully supervised learning approaches. Masked autoencoders use self-supervised learning by intentionally masking portions of input data and learning to reconstruct complete patterns from remaining information, making them well-suited for data-scarce scenarios. The significance of this work lies in improving machine learning approaches for the oil, gas, and geothermal industries to optimize drilling performance and cost-efficiency. However, limitations include the restricted dataset from only two wells and potential variations in geological characteristics and drilling conditions across different regions that could affect generalizability.

##
keyPoints: **데이터 불균형 문제** / Data Asymmetry Problem
**자기지도학습의 실용화** / Self-Supervised Learning Application
**산업적 효율성 향상** / Industrial Efficiency Improvement
**일반화 가능성의 제한** / Limited Generalizability

articles.title: FairyFuse: Multiplication-Free LLM Inference on CPUs via Fused Ternary Kernels
articles.titleKo: FairyFuse: 융합 삼진 커널을 통한 CPU 상의 곱셈-자유 LLM 추론
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.20913v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are increasingly deployed on CPU-only platforms where memory bandwidth is the primary bottleneck for autoregressive generation. Weight quantization to four bits or below reduces memory pressure, yet existing systems still dequantize weights and perform floating-point multiplications, limiting the achievable gains. Ternary weights in {-1, 0, +1} provide a more efficient alternative, replacing multiplications with conditional additions, subtractions, or no-ops. While Fairy2i shows that ternary LLMs can match FP16 quality, its
articles.aiSummary:
Large language models deployed on CPU-only platforms face significant performance challenges, with memory bandwidth being the primary bottleneck for autoregressive text generation. While existing weight quantization methods reduce weights to four bits or lower, they still require dequantization and floating-point multiplication operations, limiting efficiency gains. This research proposes FairyFuse, which uses ternary weights restricted to {-1, 0, +1} values, replacing costly multiplications with conditional additions, subtractions, or no-operations. Previous work (Fairy2i) demonstrated that ternary LLMs can maintain FP16 quality, but practical implementation efficiency remained challenging. FairyFuse addresses this gap through fused kernel implementation that dramatically accelerates CPU-based LLM inference by eliminating multiplication operations. This work has significant implications for democratizing AI by enabling deployment of high-performance language models on edge devices and resource-constrained environments. However, limitations include potential information loss from ternary quantization and the need for broader compatibility testing across diverse CPU architectures.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
대규모 언어모델(LLM)은 CPU 환경에서 메모리 대역폭이 성능의 주요 병목이 되고 있다. 기존 4비트 이하의 양자화 기법도 여전히 가중치를 부동소수점으로 변환한 후 곱셈 연산을 수행하기 때문에 효율성이 제한적이다. 이 연구는 가중치를 {-1, 0, +1} 삼진값으로 제한하고 곱셈을 조건부 덧셈/뺄셈 또는 무연산으로 대체하는 FairyFuse 기법을 제안한다. 이는 메모리 접근을 줄이고 CPU에서 더 빠른 처리를 가능하게 한다. Fairy2i 연구에서 삼진 LLM이 FP16(16비트 부동소수점) 품질을 유지할 수 있음이 입증되었으나, 실제 구현 단계에서의 효율성 문제가 남아있었다. FairyFuse는 삼진 가중치를 활용한 융합 커널(fused kernel) 구현으로 CPU 기반 LLM 추론을 비약적으로 가속화하는 것을 목표로 한다. 이 연구는 엣지 디바이스에서 고성능 AI 모델 배포를 가능하게 함으로써 AI 민주화에 기여할 수 있다. 다만 삼진 양자화 과정에서의 정보손실과 다양한 CPU 아키텍처에서의 호환성이 추가 검증이 필요한 부분이다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Large language models deployed on CPU-only platforms face significant performance challenges, with memory bandwidth being the primary bottleneck for autoregressive text generation. While existing weight quantization methods reduce weights to four bits or lower, they still require dequantization and floating-point multiplication operations, limiting efficiency gains. This research proposes FairyFuse, which uses ternary weights restricted to {-1, 0, +1} values, replacing costly multiplications with conditional additions, subtractions, or no-operations. Previous work (Fairy2i) demonstrated that ternary LLMs can maintain FP16 quality, but practical implementation efficiency remained challenging. FairyFuse addresses this gap through fused kernel implementation that dramatically accelerates CPU-based LLM inference by eliminating multiplication operations. This work has significant implications for democratizing AI by enabling deployment of high-performance language models on edge devices and resource-constrained environments. However, limitations include potential information loss from ternary quantization and the need for broader compatibility testing across diverse CPU architectures.

##
keyPoints: **곱셈 제거를 통한 효율성**: 가중치를 {-1, 0, +1} 삼진값으로 제한하여 곱셈 연산을 조건부 덧셈/뺄셈으로 대체, CPU 연산 비용을 획기적으로 감소 / **Multiplication Elimination**: Restricting weights to ternary values {-1, 0, +1} replaces expensive multiplications with conditional additions/subtractions, drastically reducing CPU computational cost

**메모리 대역폭 최적화**: CPU 환경에서 메모리 접근을 최소화하여 메모리 대역폭이라는 근본적 병목을 해결하고, 자동 회귀 생성 속도 향상 / **Memory Bandwidth Optimization**: Minimizes memory access patterns in CPU environments, addressing the fundamental memory bandwidth bottleneck and improving autoregressive generation speed

**품질-효율성 균형**: Fairy2i 연구에서 증명된 FP16 수준의 모델 품질 유지를 바탕으로, 실제 구현 단계에서 실용적인 성능 향상 달성 / **Quality-Efficiency Balance**: Builds on Fairy2i's proof that ternary LLMs maintain FP16 quality while achieving practical performance improvements in real implementation
**엣지 디바이스 배포 가능성**: CPU 기반 추론의 획기적 가속화로 고성능 LLM을 저사양 기기와

articles.title: Only Brains Align with Brains: Cross-Region Alignment Patterns Expose Limits of Normative Models
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21780v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neuroscientists and computer vision researchers use model-brain alignment benchmarks to compare artificial and biological vision systems. These benchmarks rank models according to alignment measures such as the similarity of representational geometry or the predictability of neural responses from model activations. However, recent works have identified a number of problems with these rankings, among them their lack of discriminative power and robustness, raising the conceptual question of what it means for a model to be brain-aligned. articles.title: Hierarchical organization of critical brain dynamics
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21832v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The hierarchical organization of the brain is a fundamental structural principle, while brain criticality is a leading hypothesis for its collective dynamics. However, the connection between structure and signatures of criticality remains an open question. Here, we address this issue by applying phenomenological renormalization group approaches to large-scale neuronal spiking activity from the mouse visual cortex and hippocampus. We find that signatures of criticality are not uniform, but instead vary systematically along the known anatomical hie

articles.title: Modulating Cross-Modal Convergence with Single-Stimulus, Intra-Modal Dispersion
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural networks exhibit a remarkable degree of representational convergence across diverse architectures, training objectives, and even data modalities. This convergence is predictive of alignment with brain representation. A recent hypothesis suggests this arises from learning the underlying structure in the environment in similar ways. However, it is unclear how individual stimuli elicit convergent representations across networks. An image can be perceived in multiple ways and expressed differently using words. Here, we introduce a methodology

articles.title: Micro-DualNet: Dual-Path Spatio-Temporal Network for Micro-Action Recognition
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21011v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: Micro-actions are subtle, localized movements lasting 1-3 seconds such as scratching one's head or tapping fingers. Such subtle actions are essential for social communication, ubiquitously used in natural interactions, and thus critical for fine-grained video understanding, yet remain poorly understood by current computer vision systems. We identify a fundamental challenge: micro-actions exhibit diverse spatio-temporal characteristics where some are defined by spatial configurations while others manifest through temporal dynamics. articles.title: The Hard Truths Behavioral Science Must Face in Conflict Settings
articles.summary: Approximately 2 billion people live in conflict-affected areas. Yet the field of behavioral science has developed most of its theories, run most of its experiments, and built most of its interventions in stable contexts. When behavioral scientists do work in conflict settings, we tend to follow a familiar pattern: take what worked elsewhere, “localize” it, and evaluate it. But this isn’t enough. This is an urgent problem. Conflict is reshaping lives globally at an unprecedented scale. In 2024, 123 million people were forcibly displaced —the highest number on record. Civilian casualties in Gaza

articles.title: The ‘Busy’ Trap
articles.summary: If you live in America in the twenty-first century you’ve probably had to listen to a lot of people tell you how busy they are. It’s become the default response when you ask anyone how they’re doing: “Busy!” “So busy.” “ Crazy busy.” It is, pretty obviously, a boast disguised as a complaint. And the stock response is a kind of congratulation: “That’s a good problem to have,” or “Better than the opposite.” Notice it isn’t generally people pulling back-to-back shifts in the I.C.U. or commuting by bus to three minimum-wage jobs who tell you how busy they are; what those people are is not busy but

articles.title: What It’s Like to Be…an Aerospace Engineer
articles.summary: Landing the Perseverance rover on Mars, working in clean rooms to minimize the microbial bug count, and slogging through hundreds of engineering trade-offs with Swati Mohan, an aerospace engineer at NASA's Jet Propulsion Laboratory.
